body: PT Asri Karya Lestari Tbk (ASLI) has seen its stock price drop 50% since its debut on the stock market on January 5, 2024, with its current share price at IDR 50 per share, down from IDR 100 per share at its initial public offering (IPO). The company raised IDR 125 billion from its IPO, with funds allocated to capital injections in its two subsidiaries, PT Bumi Prima Konstruksi and PT Manyar Perkasa Mandiri.
title: PT Asri Karya Lestari's stock price plummets 50% since its IPO debut in January 2024.

body: The top five gainers on the Indonesia Stock Exchange in December 2024, compared to November 2024, were PT GeoPrima Solusi Tbk (GPSS), PT Aesler Grup Internasional Tbk (RONY), PT Exploitasi Energi Indonesia Tbk (CNKO), PT Krida Jaringan Nusantara Tbk (KJEN), and PT Techno9 Indonesia Tbk (NINE), with stock price increases ranging from 207.14% to 244.10%. The highest increase was seen in GPSS, with a 244.10% surge to IDR 1,225 per share, followed by RONY with a 242.45% increase to IDR 1,815 per share.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Sees Top Five Gainers in December 2024 with Significant Stock Price Increases

body: Foreign capital inflow into Indonesia's capital markets reached IDR 212.39 trillion in 2024, with net purchases of IDR 15.74 trillion in stocks, IDR 34.59 trillion in government bonds, and IDR 161.99 trillion in Bank Indonesia rupiah securities. Between December 30, 2024, and January 2, 2025, net buy volume in the stock market was IDR 320 billion, IDR 1.94 trillion in SBN, and IDR 1.17 trillion in SRBI, with the rupiah trading at IDR 16,197 per dollar.
title: Foreign Capital Inflows into Indonesia's Capital Markets Reach IDR 212.39 Trillion in 2024

body: Leo Andyanto, the ultimate beneficial owner of PT Leyand International Tbk (LAPD), increased his stake to 7.52% by purchasing 70 million shares worth IDR 1.3 billion, bringing his total shares to 298.32 million. This transaction increased his portion from 5.76% to 7.52% of the company's total shares, which stand at 3.96 billion.
title: Leo Andyanto increases stake in PT Leyand International to 7.52% following IDR 1.3 billion transaction.

body: Pemberton Asian Opportunities Fund increased its stake in PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk (PZZA) to 8.01% after buying 32 million shares at IDR 113 per share, valued at IDR 3.61 billion, and a previous purchase of 40 million shares at IDR 128 per share, totaling IDR 5.12 billion. The fund's total shares now stand at 242 million, up from 210 million, with ultimate beneficiary Alwin Arifin holding a 64.79% controlling stake and 35.21% non-controlling ownership.
title: Pemberton Asian Opportunities Fund increases stake in PT Sarimelati Kencana to over 8% through recent share purchases.

body: PT Humpuss Maritim Internasional Tbk (HUMI) has signed a 5-year vessel charter contract with PT Pelayaran Maritim Hijau, with a prepaid charter worth USD 2 million, and has the option to purchase the vessel for USD 1.5 million at the end of the contract period. As of September 30, 2024, HUMI had cash and cash equivalents of USD 51 million, with revenue of USD 92 million and a net profit of USD 8 million.
title: Humpuss Maritime subsidiary signs 5-year vessel charter contract with purchase option.

body: PT Intikeramik Alamasri Industri Tbk (IKAI) projects a 8.7% year-over-year revenue decline to IDR 190 billion in 2024, citing political uncertainties and foreign exchange rate fluctuations as key factors. Despite this, the company remains optimistic about a 20-25% revenue growth in 2025, allowing it to maintain its compounded gross margin at the same level.
title: PT Intikeramik Alamasri Industri's revenue expected to decline 8.7% in 2024 due to political uncertainties and foreign exchange rate fluctuations.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) surged 1.82% or 127.86 points to 7,036.57, driven by gains in PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN), and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS), which contributed 33.34, 18.22, and 17.34 points to the index, respectively. Meanwhile, PT Bank Mega Tbk (MEGA), PT Adaro Resources Indonesia Tbk (ADRO), and PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk (INTP) weighed down the index, with declines of 3.60, 2.55, and 1.89 points, respectively.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Rises 1.82% to 7,036.57 on Strong Gains in Select Stocks.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 279.56 million shares on Friday, January 3, with PT Bumi Resources Minerals TBK (BUMI) and PT Bumi Resources TBK (BUMI) ranking top in the sell list, while PT Maharaksa Biru Energi TBK (MBEN) saw the largest net buy of 67.02 million shares. Meanwhile, PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (GOTO) recorded a significant net purchase of 52.57 million shares, and PT Merdeka Battery Materials TBK (MBBM) closed the top buy list with a net buy of 20.38 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell 3.72 billion shares, buy 3.44 billion shares in Indonesian market on Friday.

body: PT Multi Medika Internasional Tbk (MMIX) has partnered with Fujian Nice Paper Hygiene Products Co., China, to establish a diaper factory in Indonesia through a joint venture, with the capital investment amount yet to be determined. The joint venture is expected to increase MMIX's revenues in the future, which currently stands at IDR 93.18 billion as of September 2024, down from IDR 139.83 billion in the same period in 2023.
title: PT Multi Medika Internasional partners with Chinese company to establish diaper factory in Indonesia.

body: International tourist arrivals in Indonesia rose 17.29% to 1.09 million in November 2024, with Malaysia being the top country of origin, accounting for 174,265 visits, a 22.94% increase from November 2023. Tourist visits from ASEAN countries reached 383,038, the highest among all regions, followed by Asia with 319,508 visits, and Europe with 158,444 visits.
title: International tourist arrivals in Jakarta increase by 17.29% in November 2024, with Malaysia being the top country of origin.

body: The Financial Services Authority (OJK) will take over the supervisory role of crypto assets from the Commodity Futures Trading Regulatory Agency on January 10, 2025, following the publication of required regulations, with the government regulation bill expected to be passed soon. The transition of Bappebti from the Ministry of Trading to OJK is also underway, with officials assuring that it will not pose major challenges and will not obstruct existing license services previously authorized by Bappebti.
title: Indonesian Financial Services Authority to Take Over Crypto Asset Supervision from Commodity Futures Trading Regulatory Agency in 2025
